pure white anchored. Max deviation from identity is ~9,800 at i≈75,
well within the 32,000 driver safety limit.

commit.message:
types: re-record range bound spans with inferred element type (#634)

For-loop range literals like `0..10` are synthesized with a fresh TypeVar
for the element type so the loop body can constrain it (e.g. from an
`i32` accumulator).  The *range* TypeVar was already unified correctly,
but the *bound literal spans* (the `0` and `10`) were permanently
recorded as `I64` by the initial `synthesize` call.

Because codegen uses `resolvedTypeOf(span)` in `generateLiteral` to
choose the MLIR constant width, this caused two issues:
1. Bounds were always emitted as i64 constants even when the range was
   inferred to be `Range<i32>`, producing unnecessarily wide IR.
2. A bound that doesn't fit in the inferred type (e.g. `0..300` with
   `i8` constraint) would silently generate a truncated constant —
   e.g. 300 → 44 — causing wrong iteration counts at runtime.

Fix:
- Add `deferred_range_bounds: Vec<(Span, TypeVar, Option<i64>)>` to
  Checker, populated in `check_binary_op` when both bounds are coercible
  integer literals.
- Add `apply_deferred_range_bound_types` which runs after
  `default_unconstrained_range_types` in `check_program`.  It resolves
  each `TypeVar` to its concrete type, validates the literal fits (emitting
  an error if not), and re-records the bound span with the correct type.
- Add a `check_against` arm for `(Expr::Binary{Range|RangeInclusive},
  Range<T>)` so that range literals in typed positions (function args,
  `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`) check the bounds directly against `T`.

Tests:
- `for_range_infers_i32_from_body_usage`: verifies bound spans are
  re-recorded as I32 (not I64) when body constrains the induction variable.
- `range_literal_check_against_range_i32`: range literal coerces to a
  `Range<i32>` parameter without errors.
- `for_range_unconstrained_defaults_to_i64`: unconstrained range still
  defaults to i64 (no regression).
- `for_range_bound_overflow_errors`: `0..300` with an i8 constraint now
  correctly errors instead of silently truncating.
- `range_literal_assigned_to_range_i32`: `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add SSH config generator to user profile page (#3)

* Implement Callis SSH bastion host with web management UI

Complete implementation of the Callis application:
- Hardened OpenSSH sshd container (Alpine, Ed25519 only, AuthorizedKeysCommand)
- FastAPI web app with Jinja2 + htmx UI (Pico CSS, no build step)
- User management with RBAC (admin/operator/readonly)
- SSH public key management with validation (Ed25519, RSA >= 4096)
- Mandatory TOTP 2FA enrollment for all users
- Append-only audit logging of all actions
- Internal key endpoint on port 8081 (Docker-network only)
- Security middleware (CSP, session, TOTP guard, rate limiting)
- Docker Compose orchestration with fail2ban sidecar
- Full README with setup instructions for self-hosters

https://claude.ai/code/session_0184uJ8niU8pCv2WsYwQoR85

* Fix all 18 Copilot review comments: security, correctness, and quality

Security:
- Use HTTPS_ENABLED for secure cookie flag in auth.py and session middleware
- Disable AllowAgentForwarding in sshd_config
- Add permitopen enforcement in internal.py based on user-host assignments
- Add server-side username validation with reserved name checks in users.py
- Validate role parameter with 400 response instead of unhandled ValueError

Correctness:
- Fix naive vs aware datetime comparison in audit.py date filters
- Fix HTMX branch in users.py to return correct template with matching context
- Use urlparse for ssh_host extraction in main.py and hosts.py
- Fix ProxyJump snippets in host_row.html and hosts.html to use parsed ssh_host
- Remove inaccurate last_used_at update from internal.py keys endpoint
- Show audit nav link for all roles (consistent with backend access control)

Performance/Quality:
- Replace N+1 key count queries with single GROUP BY aggregate in users.py
- Precompute dummy bcrypt hash at startup for constant-time login checks
- Remove redundant sed in sshd Dockerfile (overwritten by COPY)
- Guard .pub file chmod in entrypoint.sh, regenerate if missing
- Configure sshd to log to file (-E) so fail2ban can read auth logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send (#609)

* fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(types): Actor<T> handle is always Send/Sync at spawn-capture boundary

The Send check for spawned lambda actor captures did not recognise the
built-in Actor<T> type (returned by 'spawn (x: T) => { ... }') as Send.
Only user-defined actors in self.actors were tested; Actor<T> fell
through to the field-based check and was incorrectly rejected.

This broke the pipeline pattern where one lambda actor captures another:

    let printer = spawn (x: int) => { println(x); };
    let doubler = spawn (x: int) => { printer.send(x * 2); };
                                     ^^^^^^^ Actor<int> capture

Add an explicit arm alongside ActorRef that marks Actor<T> as
Send + Sync + Clone + Debug. Add a regression test.

commit.message:
fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked (#633)

* fix(types): mark implicit regex import used on RegexLiteral synthesis

The CLI injects a synthetic `import std::text::regex` when regex literals
are detected in source.  The type checker stores the import's short name
(`regex`) in `import_spans` but, when synthesising the `regex.Pattern`
type for a `RegexLiteral` expression, never inserted `regex` into
`used_modules`.  This caused the end-of-program unused-import sweep to
emit a false-positive "unused import: regex" warning for every file that
contained regex literals (e.g. examples/regex_demo.hew).

Fix: in the `Expr::RegexLiteral` arm of `synthesize_inner`, call
`self.used_modules.borrow_mut().insert("regex")` before returning the
`Ty::Named { name: "regex.Pattern" }` type, mirroring what the named-
module-call path already does for other stdlib modules.

Regression test added in `hew-types/tests/e2e_typecheck.rs`: compiles a
minimal program with an explicit `import std::text::regex` and a regex
literal, then asserts no `UnusedImport` warning is present.

* refactor(hew-observe): extract TraceEvent::is_actionable() predicate

The four-way event-type check ("send" | "spawn" | "crash" | "stop") was
duplicated verbatim in both app.rs (trace navigation scroll) and ui.rs
(timeline rendering).  Extract it as a method on TraceEvent in client.rs —
the type that owns the field — so there is a single source of truth.

Zero behaviour change: same four strings, same filter semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked

The compile_from_source_checked function (used by hew eval / REPL path)
called serialize_to_msgpack with 6 arguments, omitting drop_funcs between
handle_type_repr and source_path. The function signature requires 7 args:

  serialize_to_msgpack(program, expr_types, handle_types,
                       handle_type_repr, drop_funcs,  ← missing
                       source_path, line_map)

The first call site (compile(), ~line 701) was already correct.
Only compile_from_source_checked (~line 805) was broken.

Fix: thread meta.drop_funcs into the call, restoring drop-function
metadata parity between the two compile paths.

---------

commit.message:
fix(runtime/wasm): restore actor arena lifecycle parity (#617)

* fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

The WASM scheduler's activate_actor_wasm did not call set_current_arena
before dispatch or hew_arena_reset after it, leaving a parity gap with
the native scheduler.

Changes:
- Add arena_install / arena_reset shim fns with cfg guards:
  non-WASM calls real crate::arena fns; wasm32 uses no-op stubs (WASM-TODO)
- In activate_actor_wasm:
  - Replace PREV_ARENA=null with PREV_ARENA=arena_install(a.arena) so the
    actor's arena is installed before dispatch and the prior arena is saved
  - Cache actor_arena before the dispatch loop (avoids dangling read if
    terminate_fn frees the actor)
  - After the loop: let _ = arena_install(PREV_ARENA) to restore the
    previous arena (discard return explicit), then arena_reset(actor_arena)
    with a comment explaining unconditional reset is safe on WASM because
    there is no siglongjmp crash path that could issue a competing reset
- reset_globals: clear thread-local current arena for test isolation
- Add four focused tests: install+clear, cursor reset, reentrant restore,

* fix(ci): correct tar stdin flag for gitleaks binary extraction

tar xz gitleaks -C /tmp reads from a file named 'gitleaks', not stdin.
Use tar -xzf - -C /tmp gitleaks to explicitly read from stdin (-f -)
and extract only the gitleaks member to /tmp.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): use bash array for gitleaks SCAN_ARGS to prevent word-splitting

Using a plain string SCAN_ARGS and unquoted $SCAN_ARGS expansion causes
shell word-splitting: '--log-opts=--no-merges --first-parent BASE..HEAD'
splits into three tokens, making '--first-parent' an unknown gitleaks flag.

Switch to a bash array so gitleaks receives '--log-opts' and the full
'--no-merges --first-parent BASE..HEAD' string as two properly-quoted args.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
